data.title: Americas Gold and Silver: Buy Rating Reiterated, $9.75 Price Target Maintained on Operational Improvements and Galena Shaft Modernization
data.description:
Heiko Ihle, an analyst from H.C. Wainwright, reiterated the Buy rating on Americas Gold and Silver. The associated price target remains the same with $9.75.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Heiko Ihle has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore Americas Gold and Silver’s improving operational profile and growth potential. The company’s second-quarter 2026 results showed solid silver-equivalent production despite a temporary disruption at Galena, and management reaffirmed full-year silver guidance with a clear bias toward stronger output in the second half of the year, supported by a robust cash position to fund ongoing reinvestment.

A key pillar of the thesis is the completion of Phase 2 of the No. 3 Shaft modernization at Galena, which materially enhances hoisting and throughput capacity and is expected to drive higher production, lower unit costs, and greater leverage to silver prices as the mine ramps up. Ihle’s valuation, based on a discounted cash flow approach with differentiated discount rates for each asset and a 2.0x NAV multiple in line with comparable peers, supports maintaining a $9.75 price target, reinforcing his conviction in the stock’s upside potential and justifying the reiterated Buy rating.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Daniel Brennan Boosts Thermo Fisher Price Target to $675 on Strengthening Recovery and Improving Earnings Power
data.description:
Analyst Daniel Brennan from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Thermo Fisher and increased the price target to $675.00 from $625.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Daniel Brennan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors pointing to a strengthening recovery at Thermo Fisher. The company delivered a solid second quarter with better-than-expected organic growth and raised full-year guidance, signaling that improving trends in pharma, biotech, industrial and semiconductor end markets are now feeding through to results.

Brennan also highlights that Thermo Fisher’s margin performance and earnings power are improving, supporting a higher valuation multiple as demand continues to rebound. Reflecting these dynamics, he increased his price target from $625 to $675, based on stronger forward earnings estimates and confidence that the company can sustain accelerating organic growth over the next several years.

In another report released today, Bank of America Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $650.00 price target.

data.title: Union Pacific: Strong Q2 Beat, Improved Network Efficiency and CN Partnership Drive Buy Rating and Price Target Increase from $282 to $325
data.description:
In a report released today, Jason Seidl from TD Cowen reiterated a Buy rating on Union Pacific, with a price target of $325.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Jason Seidl has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Union Pacific’s strong operational and financial execution. The company delivered second‑quarter earnings and revenue that exceeded both his estimates and market consensus, powered by broad-based volume growth and improved efficiency metrics, and management raised earnings guidance, signaling confidence in the outlook.

Jason Seidl’s rating is based on his view that traffic trends and intermodal conversions should sustain momentum in the second half, while the network remains fluid with better dwell times, train speeds, and car velocity. He also sees strategic upside from Union Pacific’s agreement with CN, which meaningfully improves shipper optionality around the pending merger, and he reflects this more constructive stance by revising his price target upward from $282 to $325.

In another report released today, Bank of America Securities also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $334.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Jason Seidl Reiterates Buy on Norfolk Southern, Raising Price Target to $376 After Strong Q2 and Operational Improvements
data.description:
In a report released today, Jason Seidl from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Norfolk Southern, with a price target of $376.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Jason Seidl has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Norfolk Southern’s solid second-quarter performance, where earnings and operating metrics surpassed both his own forecasts and broader market expectations. He also raised his price target to $376, reflecting confidence that stronger carload volumes, improving yields, and favorable coal and industrial trends can support further upside.

Moreover, Seidl highlights that management is proactively addressing recent service challenges by installing a new chief operating officer and implementing operational changes that are already improving train velocity and on-time performance. With robust demand anticipated across key freight segments in the second half and ongoing reindustrialization driving incremental volume, he sees a supportive backdrop for margin enhancement and earnings growth, justifying the Buy recommendation.

Seidl covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Knight Transportation, Union Pacific, and CH Robinson. According to TipRanks, Seidl has an average return of 27.0% and a 71.22% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $365.00 price target.

data.title: Muted Growth and Rising Underwriting Pressure Prompt Hold Rating on RLI Shares
data.description:
William Blair analyst Adam Klauber has maintained their neutral stance on RLI stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Adam Klauber has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors around RLI’s profitability and growth profile. The company’s recent earnings beat was driven largely by favorable reserve releases, while core underwriting metrics deteriorated as the underlying loss ratio and expense ratio both moved higher, signaling rising pressure on the combined ratio.

At the same time, premium growth is slowing, with property business facing persistent headwinds and casualty rate momentum decelerating, leading to an expectation of only low single‑digit top-line expansion and a notable EPS decline in 2026. Although RLI is still a strong performer versus peers and should sustain solid margins over the long run, the combination of muted growth, increasing underwriting pressure, and management’s cautious stance supports maintaining a neutral, or Hold, recommendation on the shares.

According to TipRanks, Klauber is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 2.2% and a 50.89% success rate. Klauber covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Progressive, Ryan Specialty Group, and HCI Group.

data.title: Ryan Merkel Maintains Hold on Pool as In-Line Results and Mixed Margin Trends Support Balanced Risk-Reward
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their neutral stance on POOL stock, giving a Hold rating on July 20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ryan Merkel has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Pool’s current performance and outlook. The company delivered results and guidance in line with already muted expectations, easing fears that management would sharply cut forecasts, yet not providing enough upside surprise to warrant a more positive stance.

At the same time, cost discipline and SG&A leverage are encouraging, signaling some operational improvement, but pressure on gross margins from higher freight costs and mix remains a concern. With demand trends characterized by steady maintenance activity but sluggish discretionary spending, Merkel sees a balanced risk‑reward profile, supporting a neutral, or Hold, recommendation rather than a more decisive call.

In another report released on July 20, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $235.00 price target.
